answer:
The Smartsheet API uses OAuth 2.0 and API access tokens for authentication [1][2]. All API requests require an Authorization HTTP header containing a Bearer token, formatted as shown below [3][4]:

`Authorization: REDACTED_EXAMPLE`

For machine-to-machine integrations, you can generate a personal access token directly in the Smartsheet UI under Personal Settings [3][5]. For third-party applications requiring user consent and interaction, you must implement a 3-legged OAuth 2.0 flow to retrieve access tokens on behalf of users [3][6][7]. Both methods utilize the same Authorization header structure for API requests [3][1].

results.title: 5 steps for getting started with the Smartsheet API | Smartsheet
